key_equations: \frac{\partial p_A}{\partial t} = D_{\text{eff}}\frac{\partial^2 p_A}{\partial x^2} - g\frac{4RT}{h\,N_0}
F(x,t) = -HW \frac{D_{\mathrm{eff}}}{k_B T} \frac{d p_A}{d x} \approx \rho_A H W \frac{D_{\mathrm{eff}}}{k_B T} \frac{1}{x_s}
key_relations: diffusion_PDE: p_A evolution depends_on D_eff, x, g, h, N_0, R, T
steady_diffusion: 0 ≈ D_eff * ∂²p_A/∂x² links D_eff, p_A, x

sections.diffusion: Diffusion of reactant A is governed by an effective diffusion coefficient D_eff that combines molecular diffusion D_A and Knudsen diffusion D_Kn via a harmonic sum. D_A depends on the molecular speed v_A and the collision-related parameter z_A; D_Kn depends on the hydraulic diameter h and gas properties. The pressure field p_A(x,t) follows a diffusion–reaction equation ∂p_A/∂t = D_eff ∂²p_A/∂x² − g 4RT/(h N_0). Under quasi-steady assumptions D_eff ∂²p_A/∂x² ≈ 0, giving an approximately linear p_A(x) profile in the penetrated region.
sections.adsorption: Surface adsorption and desorption are described by fluxes f_ads and f_des. Adsorption is given by f_ads = (1−θ) c N_0 p_A / √(2π M_A R T), decreasing with coverage θ and depending on gas pressure, temperature, and sticking coefficient c. Desorption is modeled as f_des = q P_0 d_s, proportional to the areal site density q. Net adsorption rate g = f_ads − f_des drives the coverage evolution via dθ/dt = g/q. At equilibrium (g = 0), θ_eq and p_A define a Langmuir-type equilibrium constant K, which can also be expressed in terms of microscopic parameters c, Q, q, and desorption factors.
sections.geometry: Geometry enters mainly through the channel height H and width W. These define an effective hydraulic diameter h = (1/2) H W / (H + W), which determines the Knudsen diffusion coefficient D_Kn. The same H and W also scale the diffusive molar flow F(x,t) ≈ ρ_A H W D_eff/(k_B T) · 1/x_s through the cross-sectional area. The saturation front position x_s sets the length over which diffusion occurs. Molecular size d_A is derived from bulk properties (M_A, N_0, ρ, R, T) and feeds into collision-based transport via z_A and D_A.
sections.saturation: Saturation is represented by the surface coverage θ and the moving front position x_s. Within the penetrated region x < x_s, the gas-phase pressure decays linearly as p_A(x,t) = p_A0(1 − x/x_s). The net adsorption rate g and capacity parameter q control how fast θ approaches its equilibrium value θ_eq. The equilibrium constant K = (θ_eq/p_A)/(1−θ_eq) connects θ_eq to p_A and to microscopic parameters through K = c Q/(q P_0 d_s). Together, q, g, and x_s determine how quickly and how far into the structure surfaces become saturated during exposure.
